commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a UTF-8 byte cap to embedding text construction and regression tests for byte limits, surrogate-pair truncation, and a dense publish bundle shape.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main builds a 12,000-character embedding string from SKILL.md plus selected text files and sends it to `generateEmbedding`, where an OpenAI 4xx input-limit response aborts publish. I did not live-replay the exact reporter bundle.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real-behavior proof of the affected publish path, not an automation-safe code repair.

target.title: fix(clawhub): cap embedding text by bytes
target.body:
## Summary
- cap publish embedding input with a conservative UTF-8 byte budget before calling the embedding provider
- keep the existing character cap, then truncate on code-point/byte boundaries so multibyte text is not split
- add regression coverage for byte-limited truncation, surrogate-pair-safe truncation, and a dense publish bundle shaped like the reported failure

Fixes #2314

## Validation
- `bun run test -- convex/lib/skills.test.ts convex/lib/embeddings.test.ts convex/lib/skillPublish.test.ts` - 3 files passed, 42 tests
- `bun run ci:unit` - 208 files passed, 2061 tests
- `bun run ci:static` - passed
- `bun run ci:types-build` - passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
target.author: pragnyanramtha
